scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within biostatistics and data visualization scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Inverse variance weighting for pooled effect is correct; OR <= 0 and inverted CI validation documented; SE from CI width using 1.96 is standard

code_usability.detail: Script requires matplotlib/numpy/pandas; syntax valid; logic sound; OR and CI validation documented in Error Handling

functional_suitability.note: OR <= 0 and ci_lower >= ci_upper validation documented with exact error messages; --demo flag documented; subgroup label limitation documented; script-level enforcement still unverified

reliability.note: OR and CI validation documented; --demo flag for testing; subgroup label gap documented in Error Handling; zero CI width (SE=0) still not handled

performance_context.note: Token usage is proportional to input complexity; execution overhead is acceptable for biostatistics and data visualization tasks though room for compression exists.

agent_usability.note: Fallback template documented; CSV format documented; style presets documented; --demo flag documented; subgroup label limitation documented in Error Handling

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical biostatistics and data visualization use cases.

security.note: No credential concerns; file paths not sanitized but no code execution risk

maintainability.note: Well-structured with separate functions; 212 lines SKILL.md; good separation of concerns; Notes section documents known limitations

agent_specific.note: Trigger description precise; CSV format documented; --demo flag documented; OR label clipping documented as known limitation; subgroup label gap documented

inputs.label: Basic forest plot from CSV with 5 studies
inputs.note: Script requires matplotlib/numpy/pandas; logic reviewed from source. Inverse variance weighting and pooled OR calculation correct. OR <= 0 validation documented.

assertions.text: Pooled OR uses inverse variance weighting
assertions.note: weights = 1/se^2; pooled = sum(w*log_or)/sum(w) is correct

assertions.text: SE is estimated from CI width as (log(ci_upper) - log(ci_lower)) / (2*1.96)
assertions.note: Standard approximation confirmed

assertions.text: OR <= 0 validation is documented with exact error message
assertions.note: Error Handling: 'If any or value is <= 0, reject with: Error: OR values must be > 0'

assertions.text: --demo flag is documented for testing without CSV input
assertions.note: SKILL.md Demo Mode section documents --demo flag

inputs.label: Forest plot with subgroup analysis
inputs.note: Subgroup analysis logic reviewed; pooled effect per subgroup calculated correctly. Subgroup label limitation documented in Error Handling.

assertions.text: Subgroup pooled effects are calculated separately
assertions.note: calculate_summary_effect() called per subgroup

assertions.text: Subgroup summary diamonds are drawn at correct y positions
assertions.note: y_pos = y_positions[mask].min() - 0.5

assertions.text: Overall pooled effect includes all studies
assertions.note: calculate_summary_effect(df) called on full dataframe

assertions.text: Subgroup label limitation is documented in Error Handling
assertions.note: Error Handling notes subgroup label rendering gap

inputs.label: Dark style preset with custom colors
inputs.note: Style presets correctly implemented. OR label clipping documented as known limitation in Notes section.

assertions.text: Dark style sets background to #1E1E1E
assertions.note: apply_style('dark') returns correct colors

assertions.text: Custom --point-color is applied to OR points
assertions.note: args.point_color passed to ax.scatter()

assertions.text: Output DPI is set to 300 by default
assertions.note: --dpi default=300 confirmed

assertions.text: OR label clipping risk is documented as known limitation
assertions.note: SKILL.md Notes section documents OR label clipping for wide CI ranges

inputs.label: Missing required columns in CSV
inputs.note: validate_data() correctly raises ValueError for missing columns. OR <= 0 and ci_lower >= ci_upper validation documented in Error Handling.

assertions.text: Missing required columns raise ValueError with column names
assertions.note: validate_data() raises ValueError with missing column list

assertions.text: Error is caught and printed to stderr
assertions.note: main() wraps in try/except and prints to stderr

assertions.text: OR <= 0 validation is documented in Error Handling
assertions.note: SKILL.md Error Handling documents exact error message

assertions.text: ci_lower >= ci_upper validation is documented in Error Handling
assertions.note: SKILL.md Error Handling documents exact error message

inputs.label: Large dataset with 20 studies and multiple subgroups
inputs.note: Auto-height calculation scales with number of studies. OR and CI validation documented but not verified in script at runtime.

assertions.text: Auto-height calculation scales with number of studies
assertions.note: --height auto scales figure height based on number of rows

assertions.text: OR <= 0 validation is enforced at runtime for all 20 studies
assertions.note: Documentation updated but validate_data() not verified to check OR > 0 in script; gap persists across four rounds

assertions.text: ci_lower >= ci_upper validation is enforced at runtime
assertions.note: Documentation updated but validate_data() not verified to check CI ordering in script; gap persists across four rounds

assertions.text: Subgroup labels appear on y-axis for each subgroup
assertions.note: Subgroup label limitation documented; users informed of gap

key_strengths: Inverse variance weighting for pooled effect size correctly implemented using log-scale OR
OR <= 0 and ci_lower >= ci_upper validation documented in Error Handling with exact error messages
Three style presets (default, minimal, dark) provide publication-ready aesthetics
Subgroup label limitation documented in Error Handling, closing the silent-failure gap

recommendations.title: OR <= 0 and inverted CI validation documented but not enforced in script after four rounds
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md Error Handling documents exact error messages for OR <= 0 and ci_lower >= ci_upper, but validate_data() in the script has not been verified to enforce these checks. This gap has persisted across all four audit rounds.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation updated across multiple audit rounds but script not verified to match.
recommendations.fix: Add to validate_data(): check that all 'or' values > 0 and all 'ci_lower' < 'ci_upper'. Raise ValueError with specific row indices for violations matching the documented error messages.

recommendations.title: Subgroup labels not shown on y-axis
recommendations.problem: When --subgroup is used, subgroup names are not added to the y-axis labels; only study names and 'Overall' appear, making it impossible to identify which subgroup each summary diamond belongs to.
recommendations.root_cause: study_labels list only appends 'Overall'; no subgroup name labels are inserted.
recommendations.fix: For each subgroup, insert a subgroup label row in study_labels at the appropriate y position, styled differently (e.g., bold or indented) to distinguish from study labels.

recommendations.title: OR labels may be placed outside figure bounds
recommendations.problem: OR labels are positioned at ax.get_xlim()[1] * 1.02, which may place them outside the figure bounds for wide CI ranges.
recommendations.root_cause: Label position is calculated relative to x-axis limits without accounting for figure margins.
recommendations.fix: Use a fixed offset from the rightmost CI upper bound rather than the axis limit, or add a right margin to the figure to accommodate labels.
